subject_options: Example Cafe is not showing up in Westport searches
A simple online presence for Example Cafe
What Westport visitors find when they search for you
email_body:
I came across Example Cafe while in Westport recently and wanted to reach out directly.

Searching for cafes in Westport online, the business does not appear in Google Maps or local search results at all. For a town with the level of visitor footfall Westport sees, that is a meaningful gap — most people search on their phones before deciding where to eat.

I help hospitality businesses in Ireland build straightforward, mobile-first websites paired with a Google Business Profile, so they show up when people are actively looking. For Example Cafe, that would mean a four-page website and a fully set-up Google listing, typically delivered within two to four weeks.

Would you have 15 minutes this week for a quick call so I can show you exactly what a Google Business Profile and a simple website would look like for Example Cafe?

Best regards
[Your name]
cta: Would you have 15 minutes this week for a quick call so I can show you exactly what a Google Business Profile and a simple website would look like for Example Cafe?
follow_up_body: I wanted to follow up on my email from last week about getting Example Cafe visible in local search results. I know this kind of thing is not always front of mind when you are running a busy cafe. The core offer is a mobile-first website and a Google Business Profile set up together, usually within two to four weeks. Is this something you would consider for the coming months, or is there someone else I should speak to about it?
personalisation_tokens_used: Westport, Co. Mayo — a tourist-active town used to frame the specific impact of having no online presence during peak visitor seasons
Lead identified through local knowledge and a physical visit — used to ground the opening line as a genuine local observation rather than a data-driven cold approach
